Array

Basic linear data structure

0) Concept

  • Java Array
    • Low level : continuous blocks in memory space

  • Types

    • greedy.md
    • matrix.md

    • index op
    • array op
    • sorting
    • binary search

    • dict

0-2) Pattern

1) General form

1-1) Basic OP

1-1-0) Split Array

1# python_trick.html
2
3#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4# example 7 : itertools.islice : slice on iterator
5#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6# https://docs.python.org/3/library/itertools.html#itertools.islice
7# syntax : itertools.islice(seq, [start,] stop [, step])
8
9In [6]:  x = itertools.islice(range(10), 0, 9, 2)
10
11In [7]: print (list(x))
12[0, 2, 4, 6, 8]
13
14
15In [18]: y = itertools.islice(range(10), 0, 10, 3)
16    ...: print (list(y))
17[0, 3, 6, 9]

1-1-1) Insert into Array

1p=[[7, 0], [7, 1], [6, 1], [5, 0], [5, 2], [4, 4]]
2Out[27]: [[7, 0], [7, 1], [6, 1], [5, 0], [5, 2], [4, 4]]
3In [28]: p.insert(1, [6,1])
4In [29]: p
5Out[29]: [[7, 0], [6, 1], [7, 1], [6, 1], [5, 0], [5, 2], [4, 4]]

1-1-2) Delete from Array

1p=[[7, 0], [7, 1], [6, 1], [5, 0], [5, 2], [4, 4]]
2
3In [4]: p
4Out[4]: [[7, 0], [7, 1], [6, 1], [5, 0], [5, 2], [4, 4]]
5
6In [5]: p.remove([7, 1])
7
8In [6]: p
9Out[6]: [[7, 0], [6, 1], [5, 0], [5, 2], [4, 4]]

1-1-3) check if element in Array

1In [7]: p
2Out[7]: [[7, 0], [6, 1], [5, 0], [5, 2], [4, 4]]
3
4In [8]: [7,0] in p
5Out[8]: True

1-1-4) append to array (head, tail)

1# tail
2In [9]: p
3Out[9]: [[7, 0], [6, 1], [5, 0], [5, 2], [4, 4]]
4
5In [10]: p.append([0,0])
6
7In [11]: p
8Out[11]: [[7, 0], [6, 1], [5, 0], [5, 2], [4, 4], [0, 0]]

1-1-5) Sort Array*****

1# Pattern :
2# V1
3_array.sort(key = lambda x : <your_sorting_func>)
4
5# V2
6sorted(_array, key = lambda x : <your_sorting_func>)
7
8# 049  Group Anagrams
9strs = ["eat","tea","tan","ate","nat","bat"]
10strs.sort(key = lambda x : ''.join(sorted(x)) )
11print (strs)
12# ['bat', 'eat', 'tea', 'ate', 'tan', 'nat']
13
14### NOTE can use this as well
15sorted(strs, key = lambda x : ''.join(sorted(x)))

1-1-6) Flatten Array

1# LC 341
2# V0
3class NestedIterator(object):
4
5    def __init__(self, nestedList):
6
7        self.queue = []
8        
9        def getAll(nests):
10            for nest in nests:
11                if nest.isInteger():
12                    self.queue.append(nest.getInteger())
13                else:
14                    getAll(nest.getList())
15        getAll(nestedList)
16
17    def next(self):
18
19        return self.queue.pop(0)
20
21    def hasNext(self):
22
23        return len(self.queue)
24
25# default py
26# V1
27def flatten_array(_array):
28    r = []
29    def helper(_array):
30        for i in _array:
31            if type(i) == int:
32                print (i)
33                r.append(i)
34            else:
35                helper(i)
36
37    helper(_array)
38    return r
39    
40_input = [1,0, [1,2,[4,[5,[6,[7]]]]]]#[1,[4,[6]]] #[[1,1],2,[1,1]]
41
42res = flatten_array(_input)
43print ("res = " + str(res))
44
45# V2
46# https://stackoverflow.com/questions/2158395/flatten-an-irregular-list-of-lists
47def flatten(L):
48    for item in L:
49        try:
50            yield from flatten(item)
51        except TypeError:
52            yield item
53
54r2 = flatten(_input)
55r2_ = [x for x in r2]
56print (r2_)
57
58# V3
59def flatten2(L):
60    for item in L:
61        try:
62            yield from flatten2(item)
63        except:
64            yield item
65
66r3 = flatten2(_input)
67r3_ = [x for x in r3]
68print (r3_)

1// java
2// algorithm book (labu) p.355
3//------------------------------------------
4// implement NestedInteger data structure
5//------------------------------------------
6public class NestedInteger {
7    private Integer val;
8    private List<NestedInteger> list;
9
10    public NestedInteger(Integer val){
11        this.val = val;
12        this.list = null;
13    }
14
15    public NestedInteger(List<NestedInteger> list){
16        this.list = list;
17        this.val = null;
18    }
19
20    // if saved value is integer, return true, else false
21    public boolean isIntger(){
22        return val != null;
23    }
24
25    // if saved value is integer, return it, else return null
26    public Integer getInteger(){
27        return this.val;
28    }
29
30    // if saved value is array, return it, else return null
31    public List<NestedInteger> getList(){
32        return this.list;
33    }
34
35}

1// java
2// LC 341
3// algorithm book (labu) p.357
4//-----------------------------------------------------------
5// NestedInteger solution V1 :  via tree algorithm
6//-----------------------------------------------------------
7class NestedIterator implements Iterator<Integer>{
8
9    private Iterator<Integer> it;
10
11    public NestedInteger(List<NestedInteger> nestedList){
12        // save flatten result
13        List<Integer> result = new LinkedList<>();
14
15        for (NestedInteger node: nestedList){
16            // start from each node and proceed
17            traverse(node, result);
18        }
19
20        // get result's iterator
21        this.it = result.iterator();
22    }
23
24    public Integer next(){
25        return it.next();
26    }
27
28    public boolean hasNext(){
29        return it.hasNext();
30    }
31
32    // traverse tree with root as root, and add nodes to result array
33    private void traverse(NestedInteger root, List<Integer> result){
34        if (root.isIntger()){
35            // arrive root node
36            result.add(root.getInteger());
37            return;
38        }
39
40        // traverse framework
41        for (NestedInteger child: root.getList()){
42            traverse(child, result);
43        }
44    }
45}

1// java
2// LC 341
3// algorithm book (labu) p.358
4//-----------------------------------------------------------
5// NestedInteger solution V2 :  via lazy calling
6//-----------------------------------------------------------
7public class NestedIterator implements Iterator<Integer>{
8
9    private LinkedList<NestedInteger> list;
10
11    public NestedInteger(List<NestedInteger> nestedList){
12        // use LinkedList, for good performance in below op
13        list = new LinkedList<>(nestedList);
14    }
15
16    public Integer next(){
17        // hasNext method make sure 1st element must be Integer type
18        return list.remove(0).getInteger();
19    }
20
21    public boolean hasNext(){
22        // for loop split elements in array until 1st element is Integer type
23        while (!list.isEmpty() && list.get(0).isIntger()){
24            // when 1st element is array type, go into the loop
25            List<NestedInteger> first = list.remove(0).getList();
26            // flatten 1st array, and add to "start" in ordering
27            for (int i = first.size() - 1; i >= 0; i--){
28                list.addFirst(first.get(i));
29            }  
30        }
31        return !list.isEmpty();
32    }
33}

1-1-7) Sort array with 2 keys

1y = [[7,0], [4,4], [7,1], [5,0], [6,1], [5,2]]
2print (y)
3y.sort(key = lambda x : (-x[0], x[1]))
4print (y)
5# [[7, 0], [4, 4], [7, 1], [5, 0], [6, 1], [5, 2]]
6# [[7, 0], [7, 1], [6, 1], [5, 0], [5, 2], [4, 4]]

1-1-8) go through 2 arrays (length could be different)

1#--------------------
2# example 1
3#--------------------
4
5# 2 array : s,t
6# len(s) = 10, len(t) = 7
7# or
8# len(s) = 10, len(t) = 11
9if len(s) > len(t):
10    s,t  = t,s
11
12for i in range(len(s)):
13    print (s[i], t[i])
14
15
16#--------------------
17# example 2
18#--------------------
19# LC 165
20class Solution:
21    def compareVersion(self, version1: str, version2: str) -> int:
22        nums1 = version1.split('.')
23        nums2 = version2.split('.')
24        n1, n2 = len(nums1), len(nums2)
25        
26        # NOTE here !!!
27        # compare versions
28        for i in range(max(n1, n2)):
29            i1 = int(nums1[i]) if i < n1 else 0
30            i2 = int(nums2[i]) if i < n2 else 0
31            if i1 != i2:
32                return 1 if i1 > i2 else -1
33        
34        # the versions are equal
35        return 0

1-1-9) shallow, deep copy

1# LC 670
2# V0'
3# IDEA : BRUTE FORCE
4# NOTE : there is also 2 pointers solution : 
5#        -> https://github.com/yennanliu/CS_basics/blob/master/leetcode_python/Array/maximum-swap.py#L49
6# NOTE : ans = A[:]
7#        A[:] is a `shallow copy` syntax in python,
8#        it will copy "parent obj" (not child obj) to the other instance
9#        so the changes ("parent obj" only) in original instance will NOT affect the copied instance
10# https://stackoverflow.com/questions/4081561/what-is-the-difference-between-list-and-list-in-python
11# https://github.com/yennanliu/til#20210923
12class Solution(object):
13    def maximumSwap(self, num):
14        A = list(str(num))
15        ans = A[:]
16        for i in range(len(A)):
17            for j in range(i+1, len(A)):
18                A[i], A[j] = A[j], A[i]
19                if A > ans: 
20                    ans = A[:]
21                A[i], A[j] = A[j], A[i]
22
23        return int("".join(ans))

1-2) Special Array Algorithms

1-2-1) Boyer-Moore Majority Vote Algorithm

Concept:

  • Find element(s) appearing more than ⌊n/k⌋ times in an array
  • Key Idea: Pair different elements and cancel them out
  • Majority element survives cancellation
  • Two-phase: (1) Find candidates, (2) Verify counts
  • Space: O(k) for k-1 candidates

When to Use:

  • “Find majority element” → element appearing > n/2 times
  • “Find all elements appearing more than n/3 times”
  • “Heavy hitters” or “frequent elements” problems
  • Need O(1) space (better than HashMap O(n))

Related: See streaming_algorithms.md for detailed explanation.

Pattern 1: Standard Majority Element (> n/2) - LC 169

Algorithm:

  • Maintain one candidate and count
  • When count=0, select new candidate
  • Increment count for same element, decrement for different
  • Majority element survives
1# Python - LC 169
2def majorityElement(nums):
3    """
4    Find element appearing > n/2 times
5    Time: O(n)
6    Space: O(1)
7    """
8    candidate = None
9    count = 0
10
11    # Phase 1: Find candidate
12    for num in nums:
13        if count == 0:
14            candidate = num
15            count = 1
16        elif num == candidate:
17            count += 1
18        else:
19            count -= 1  # Cancel out
20
21    # Phase 2: Verify (can skip if majority guaranteed)
22    # return candidate
23
24    # If not guaranteed, verify:
25    if nums.count(candidate) > len(nums) // 2:
26        return candidate
27    return None

1// Java - LC 169
2// V0
3// IDEA: Boyer-Moore Majority Vote
4/**
5 * Key Insight:
6 * - Pair different elements and cancel them out
7 * - Majority element will survive cancellation
8 * - Works because majority element appears > n/2 times
9 *
10 * Time: O(n)
11 * Space: O(1)
12 */
13public int majorityElement(int[] nums) {
14    /**
15     * time = O(N)
16     * space = O(1)
17     */
18    int candidate = nums[0];
19    int count = 1;
20
21    // Phase 1: Find candidate by cancellation
22    for (int i = 1; i < nums.length; i++) {
23        if (count == 0) {
24            // Start new candidate when count reaches 0
25            candidate = nums[i];
26            count = 1;
27        } else if (nums[i] == candidate) {
28            // Same element: increment count
29            count++;
30        } else {
31            // Different element: cancel out
32            count--;
33        }
34    }
35
36    // Phase 2: Verify (optional if majority guaranteed)
37    // If problem guarantees majority exists, return candidate directly
38    return candidate;
39
40    // Otherwise, verify:
41    // int actualCount = 0;
42    // for (int num : nums) {
43    //     if (num == candidate) actualCount++;
44    // }
45    // return actualCount > nums.length / 2 ? candidate : -1;
46}

Example Trace: nums = [2,2,1,1,1,2,2]

1Index | num | candidate | count | Action
2--------------------------------------------
3  0   |  2  |     2     |   1   | Initialize
4  1   |  2  |     2     |   2   | Same, increment
5  2   |  1  |     2     |   1   | Different, decrement
6  3   |  1  |     2     |   0   | Different, decrement
7  4   |  1  |     1     |   1   | Count=0, new candidate
8  5   |  2  |     1     |   0   | Different, decrement
9  6   |  2  |     2     |   1   | Count=0, new candidate
10
11Result: 2 (appears 4 times > 7/2 = 3.5)
Pattern 2: Elements Appearing > n/3 Times - LC 229

Key Insight: At most 2 elements can appear more than n/3 times.

Algorithm:

  • Maintain two candidates and two counts
  • Cancellation requires decrementing both counts
  • Must verify both candidates in phase 2
1# Python - LC 229
2def majorityElement(nums):
3    """
4    Find all elements appearing > n/3 times
5    Time: O(n)
6    Space: O(1)
7    """
8    # Phase 1: Find up to 2 candidates
9    candidate1, candidate2 = None, None
10    count1, count2 = 0, 0
11
12    for num in nums:
13        if num == candidate1:
14            count1 += 1
15        elif num == candidate2:
16            count2 += 1
17        elif count1 == 0:
18            candidate1, count1 = num, 1
19        elif count2 == 0:
20            candidate2, count2 = num, 1
21        else:
22            # Different from both: cancel out
23            count1 -= 1
24            count2 -= 1
25
26    # Phase 2: Verify candidates
27    result = []
28    for candidate in [candidate1, candidate2]:
29        if candidate is not None and nums.count(candidate) > len(nums) // 3:
30            result.append(candidate)
31
32    return result

1// Java - LC 229
2// V0
3// IDEA: Boyer-Moore Majority Vote (Generalized)
4/**
5 * Key Insight:
6 * - At most 2 elements can appear > n/3 times
7 * - Use 2 candidates and 2 counts
8 * - Cancellation decrements both counts
9 * - MUST verify both candidates
10 *
11 * Time: O(n)
12 * Space: O(1)
13 */
14public List<Integer> majorityElement(int[] nums) {
15    /**
16     * time = O(N)
17     * space = O(1)
18     */
19    int candidate1 = 0, candidate2 = 0;
20    int count1 = 0, count2 = 0;
21
22    // Phase 1: Find up to 2 candidates
23    for (int num : nums) {
24        if (num == candidate1) {
25            count1++;
26        } else if (num == candidate2) {
27            count2++;
28        } else if (count1 == 0) {
29            candidate1 = num;
30            count1 = 1;
31        } else if (count2 == 0) {
32            candidate2 = num;
33            count2 = 1;
34        } else {
35            // Different from both: cancel out
36            count1--;
37            count2--;
38        }
39    }
40
41    // Phase 2: Verify candidates (REQUIRED!)
42    count1 = 0;
43    count2 = 0;
44    for (int num : nums) {
45        if (num == candidate1) count1++;
46        else if (num == candidate2) count2++;
47    }
48
49    List<Integer> result = new ArrayList<>();
50    if (count1 > nums.length / 3) result.add(candidate1);
51    if (count2 > nums.length / 3) result.add(candidate2);
52
53    return result;
54}

Example Trace: nums = [3,2,3]

1Index | num | c1 | cnt1 | c2 | cnt2 | Action
2-------------------------------------------------
3  0   |  3  | 3  |  1   | 0  |  0   | Set candidate1
4  1   |  2  | 3  |  1   | 2  |  1   | Set candidate2
5  2   |  3  | 3  |  2   | 2  |  1   | Match candidate1
6
7Verification:
8- candidate1=3: appears 2 times > 3/3 = 1 ✓
9- candidate2=2: appears 1 time ≤ 3/3 = 1 ✗
10
11Result: [3]
Pattern 3: Generalized k-Majority (> n/k times)

Concept: For elements appearing more than n/k times, at most k-1 candidates exist.

1// Generalized Boyer-Moore for n/k threshold
2import java.util.*;
3
4class BoyerMooreGeneralized {
5    /**
6     * Find all elements appearing > n/k times
7     * time = O(N × k)
8     * space = O(k)
9     */
10    public List<Integer> majorityElement(int[] nums, int k) {
11        // At most k-1 candidates for n/k threshold
12        Map<Integer, Integer> candidates = new HashMap<>();
13
14        // Phase 1: Find up to k-1 candidates
15        for (int num : nums) {
16            if (candidates.containsKey(num)) {
17                candidates.put(num, candidates.get(num) + 1);
18            } else if (candidates.size() < k - 1) {
19                candidates.put(num, 1);
20            } else {
21                // Decrement all counts (cancellation)
22                List<Integer> toRemove = new ArrayList<>();
23                for (Map.Entry<Integer, Integer> entry : candidates.entrySet()) {
24                    int count = entry.getValue() - 1;
25                    if (count == 0) {
26                        toRemove.add(entry.getKey());
27                    } else {
28                        candidates.put(entry.getKey(), count);
29                    }
30                }
31                for (int key : toRemove) {
32                    candidates.remove(key);
33                }
34            }
35        }
36
37        // Phase 2: Verify all candidates
38        Map<Integer, Integer> counts = new HashMap<>();
39        for (int num : nums) {
40            if (candidates.containsKey(num)) {
41                counts.put(num, counts.getOrDefault(num, 0) + 1);
42            }
43        }
44
45        List<Integer> result = new ArrayList<>();
46        for (Map.Entry<Integer, Integer> entry : counts.entrySet()) {
47            if (entry.getValue() > nums.length / k) {
48                result.add(entry.getKey());
49            }
50        }
51
52        return result;
53    }
54}

Boyer-Moore: Common Mistakes & Tips

🚫 Common Mistakes:

  1. Missing Verification Phase

    1// ❌ WRONG: Assuming candidate is always majority
2return candidate;
3
4// ✅ CORRECT: Verify count (if not guaranteed)
5int actualCount = 0;
6for (int num : nums) {
7    if (num == candidate) actualCount++;
8}
9return actualCount > nums.length / 2 ? candidate : -1;

  2. Wrong Cancellation Logic for LC 229

    1// ❌ WRONG: Only checking candidate1
2if (num != candidate1) count1--;
3
4// ✅ CORRECT: Check both, reset on count=0
5if (num == candidate1) {
6    count1++;
7} else if (num == candidate2) {
8    count2++;
9} else if (count1 == 0) {
10    candidate1 = num; count1 = 1;
11} else if (count2 == 0) {
12    candidate2 = num; count2 = 1;
13} else {
14    count1--; count2--;
15}

  3. Not Handling Duplicate Candidates (LC 229)

    1// ❌ WRONG: candidate1 and candidate2 can be same initially
2
3// ✅ CORRECT: Check candidate1 first, then candidate2
4if (num == candidate1) count1++;
5else if (num == candidate2) count2++;
6// ... rest of logic

💡 Interview Tips:

  1. When to Use:

    • “Find majority element” keywords
    • Need O(1) space (vs HashMap O(n))
    • Guaranteed majority exists

  2. Talking Points:

    • “Pairing and cancellation eliminates non-majority elements”
    • “At most k-1 elements can appear more than n/k times”
    • “Two-phase: find candidates, then verify”

  3. Complexity:

    • Time: O(n) single pass (+ O(n) for verification = O(n) total)
    • Space: O(1) for standard, O(k) for generalized

Related LeetCode Problems:

Problem Difficulty Threshold Candidates Verify?
LC 169 Easy > n/2 1 Optional*
LC 229 Medium > n/3 2 Required
General - > n/k k-1 Required

*Optional if problem guarantees majority element exists.

Summary:

  • ✅ Boyer-Moore: O(n) time, O(1) space majority finding
  • ✅ Key insight: Cancellation eliminates non-majority elements
  • ✅ Two-phase: (1) Find candidates, (2) Verify counts
  • ✅ For > n/2: 1 candidate, for > n/3: 2 candidates, for > n/k: k-1 candidates
  • ✅ Alternative: HashMap O(n) space for exact counts

1-2-2) Frequency Array + Running Count

Concept:

  • Track element occurrences using a frequency array (when values are bounded, e.g., 1 to n)
  • Use array index as hash key for O(1) lookups
  • Maintain a running count that updates as conditions are met
  • Key Insight: When frequency reaches a threshold, trigger an action

When to Use:

  • Values are bounded/constrained (e.g., permutations with values 1 to n)
  • Need to track “seen in both” or “appeared k times” conditions
  • Prefix-based counting problems
  • Real-time/streaming count updates

Related: Useful for permutation problems, prefix arrays, and intersection counting.

Pattern: Prefix Common Array (LC 2657)

Problem: Given two permutations A and B of length n, find C where C[i] = count of numbers present at or before index i in both A and B.

Algorithm:

  • Use frequency array of size n+1 (since values are 1 to n)
  • For each index, process A[i] and B[i]
  • When any element’s frequency reaches 2, it’s common (seen in both arrays)
  • Increment running count and store in result
1# Python - LC 2657
2def findThePrefixCommonArray(A, B):
3    """
4    Find prefix common array using frequency counting.
5
6    Key Insight:
7    - Each number appears at most twice total (once in A, once in B)
8    - When count[x] == 2, x has been seen in both arrays → common element
9
10    Time: O(n)
11    Space: O(n)
12    """
13    n = len(A)
14    res = [0] * n
15    count = [0] * (n + 1)  # values are 1..n
16    common = 0
17
18    for i in range(n):
19        # Process element from A
20        count[A[i]] += 1
21        if count[A[i]] == 2:
22            common += 1
23
24        # Process element from B
25        count[B[i]] += 1
26        if count[B[i]] == 2:
27            common += 1
28
29        res[i] = common
30
31    return res

1// Java - LC 2657
2// V0
3// IDEA: Frequency Array + Running Count
4/**
5 * Core Insight:
6 * - Each number appears at most twice total (once in A, once in B)
7 * - When frequency[x] == 2, x is present in both arrays → common element
8 * - Running count tracks cumulative common elements
9 *
10 * Why This Works:
11 * - Permutation guarantee: each value 1..n appears exactly once in each array
12 * - Processing both arrays simultaneously at each index
13 * - frequency[x] can only be 0, 1, or 2
14 * - frequency[x] == 2 means: seen once in A AND once in B
15 *
16 * Time: O(n) - single pass through both arrays
17 * Space: O(n) - frequency array of size n+1
18 */
19public int[] findThePrefixCommonArray(int[] A, int[] B) {
20    int n = A.length;
21    int[] res = new int[n];
22
23    // Since values are 1 to n, use array as hash map
24    int[] frequency = new int[n + 1];
25    int commonCount = 0;
26
27    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
28        // Process element from A
29        frequency[A[i]]++;
30        if (frequency[A[i]] == 2) {
31            commonCount++;  // Now seen in both arrays
32        }
33
34        // Process element from B
35        frequency[B[i]]++;
36        if (frequency[B[i]] == 2) {
37            commonCount++;  // Now seen in both arrays
38        }
39
40        // Store current prefix common count
41        res[i] = commonCount;
42    }
43
44    return res;
45}

Example Trace: A = [1,3,2,4], B = [3,1,2,4]

1Index | A[i] | B[i] | frequency (after)     | commonCount | Action
2----------------------------------------------------------------------
3  0   |  1   |  3   | [0,1,0,1,0]           |     0       | freq[1]=1, freq[3]=1
4  1   |  3   |  1   | [0,2,0,2,0]           |     2       | freq[3]=2 ✓, freq[1]=2 ✓
5  2   |  2   |  2   | [0,2,2,2,0]           |     3       | freq[2]=1, then freq[2]=2 ✓
6  3   |  4   |  4   | [0,2,2,2,2]           |     4       | freq[4]=1, then freq[4]=2 ✓
7
8Result: [0, 2, 3, 4]
Generalized Pattern: Frequency Threshold Detection

Use this pattern when you need to detect when elements reach a specific count threshold:

1// Generalized frequency threshold pattern
2/**
3 * Detect when elements reach threshold k
4 * Useful for: intersection counting, duplicate detection, k-frequency problems
5 */
6public void frequencyThresholdPattern(int[] arr1, int[] arr2, int maxVal, int threshold) {
7    int[] frequency = new int[maxVal + 1];
8    int count = 0;
9
10    for (int i = 0; i < arr1.length; i++) {
11        // Process from first source
12        frequency[arr1[i]]++;
13        if (frequency[arr1[i]] == threshold) {
14            count++;  // Element reached threshold
15        }
16
17        // Process from second source (if applicable)
18        frequency[arr2[i]]++;
19        if (frequency[arr2[i]] == threshold) {
20            count++;
21        }
22
23        // Use count as needed...
24    }
25}

Frequency Array + Running Count: Common Mistakes & Tips

🚫 Common Mistakes:

  1. Wrong Array Size

    1// ❌ WRONG: Off-by-one for 1-indexed values
2int[] frequency = new int[n];  // Can't access frequency[n]
3
4// ✅ CORRECT: Size n+1 for values 1..n
5int[] frequency = new int[n + 1];

  2. Checking Threshold Before Increment

    1// ❌ WRONG: Check before increment misses the transition
2if (frequency[x] == 2) count++;
3frequency[x]++;
4
5// ✅ CORRECT: Increment first, then check
6frequency[x]++;
7if (frequency[x] == 2) count++;

  3. Not Handling Same Element in Both Arrays at Same Index

    1// For A[i] == B[i] case, frequency goes 0→1→2 in same iteration
2// This is handled correctly by processing A[i] then B[i] separately

💡 Interview Tips:

  1. When to Use:

    • “Permutation” or “values 1 to n” keywords
    • “Prefix” or “running” count requirements
    • “Common elements” or “intersection” problems
    • Need O(1) lookup with bounded values

  2. Talking Points:

    • “Array index as hash key gives O(1) lookup”
    • “Frequency threshold detection for condition triggers”
    • “Running count avoids recomputation”

  3. Complexity:

    • Time: O(n) single pass
    • Space: O(n) or O(max_value) for frequency array

Related LeetCode Problems:

Problem Difficulty Pattern Variant
LC 2657 Medium Prefix common array (frequency == 2)
LC 349 Easy Intersection of two arrays (frequency >= 1 in both)
LC 350 Easy Intersection with duplicates
LC 442 Medium Find duplicates (frequency == 2, in-place)
LC 448 Easy Find missing numbers (frequency == 0)
LC 645 Easy Set mismatch (frequency == 2 and == 0)
LC 1 Easy Two Sum (complement frequency check)
LC 217 Easy Contains duplicate (frequency >= 2)

Summary:

  • ✅ Frequency Array: Use array index as hash for bounded values (O(1) lookup)
  • ✅ Running Count: Maintain cumulative count, update on threshold
  • ✅ Key Insight: frequency[x] == k means x appeared k times across sources
  • ✅ Best for: Permutations, prefix problems, intersection counting
  • ✅ Alternative: HashMap for unbounded/sparse values

2) LC Example

2-1) Queue Reconstruction by Height

1# LC 406 Queue Reconstruction by Height
2class Solution(object):
3    def reconstructQueue(self, people):
4        people.sort(key = lambda x : (-x[0], x[1]))
5        res = []
6        for p in people:
7        """
8        py insert syntax
9        # syntax : 
10        # python_trick.html#1-6-insert-into-array-in-place
11
12        # arr.insert(<index>,<value>)
13        """
14            res.insert(p[1], p)
15        return res

2-2) Product of Array Except Self

1# 238 Product of Array Except Self
2# IDEA : 
3# SINCE output[i] = (x0 * x1 * ... * xi-1) * (xi+1 * .... * xn-1)
4# -> SO DO A 2 LOOP
5# -> 1ST LOOP : GO THROGH THE ARRAY (->) : (x0 * x1 * ... * xi-1)
6# -> 2ND LOOP : GO THROGH THE ARRAY (<-) : (xi+1 * .... * xn-1)
7# e.g.
8# given [1,2,3,4], return [24,12,8,6].
9# -> output = [2*3*4, 1,1,1]  <-- 2*3*4    (right of 1: 2,3,4)
10# -> output = [2*3*4, 1*3*4,1,1] <-- 1*3*4 (left of 2 :1, right of 2: 3,4)
11# -> output = [2*3*4, 1*3*4,1*2*4,1] <-- 1*2*4 (left of 3: 1,2 right of 3 : 4)
12# -> output = [2*3*4, 1*3*4,1*2*4,1*2*3] <-- 1*2*3 (left of 4 : 1,2,3)
13# -> final output  = [2*3*4, 1*3*4,1*2*4,1*2*3] = [24,12,8,6]
14class Solution:
15    def productExceptSelf(self, nums):
16        size = len(nums)
17        output = [1] * size
18        left = 1
19        for x in range(size - 1):
20            left *= nums[x]
21            output[x + 1] *= left
22        right = 1
23        for x in range(size - 1, 0, -1):
24            right *= nums[x]
25            output[x - 1] *= right
26        return output

2-3) Maximum Swap

1# 670 Maximum Swap
2class Solution(object):
3    def maximumSwap(self, num):
4        """
5        :type num: int
6        :rtype: int
7        """
8        # BE AWARE OF IT 
9        digits = list(str(num))
10        left, right = 0, 0
11        max_idx = len(digits)-1
12        for i in range(len(digits))[::-1]:
13            # BE AWARE OF IT 
14            if digits[i] > digits[max_idx]:
15                max_idx = i
16            # BE AWARE OF IT  
17            # if current digit > current max digit -> swap them 
18            elif digits[max_idx] > digits[i]:
19                left, right = i, max_idx        # if current max digit > current digit -> save current max digit to right idnex, and save current index to left
20        digits[left], digits[right] = digits[right], digits[left] # swap left and right when loop finished 
21        return int("".join(digits))

2-7) Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock

1# LC 121 Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
2# V0
3# IDEA : array op + problem understanding
4class Solution(object):
5    def maxProfit(self, prices):
6        if len(prices) == 0:
7            return 0
8        ### NOTE : we define 1st minPrice as prices[0]
9        minPrice = prices[0]
10        maxProfit = 0
11        ### NOTE : we only loop prices ONCE
12        for p in prices:
13            # only if p < minPrice, we get minPrice
14            if p < minPrice:
15                minPrice = p
16            ### NOTE : only if p - minPrice > maxProfit, we get maxProfit
17            elif p - minPrice > maxProfit:
18                maxProfit = p - minPrice
19        return maxProfit

2-8) Bulb Switcher III

1# LC 1375. Bulb Switcher III
2# V0
3class Solution:
4    def numTimesAllBlue(self, light):
5        max_bulb_ind = 0
6        count = 0
7        turnedon_bulb = 0
8        
9        for bulb in light:
10            max_bulb_ind = max(max_bulb_ind,bulb)
11            turnedon_bulb += 1
12            if turnedon_bulb == max_bulb_ind:
13                count += 1
14        
15        return count

2-9) Robot Bounded In Circle

1# LC 1041. Robot Bounded In Circle
2# V0
3# IDEA : math + array
4class Solution:
5    def isRobotBounded(self, instructions):
6        """
7        NOTE !!! we make direction as below
8
9         c == 'L':  move LEFT : [0,-1]
10         c == 'R':  move RIGHT : [0,1]
11        """
12        dirs = [[0,1], [1,0], [0,-1], [-1,0]]
13        x = 0;
14        y = 0;
15        idx = 0;
16        for c in instructions:
17            print ("c = " + str(c) + " idx = " + str(idx))
18            """
19            NOTE !!! since we need to verify if robot back to start point
20                -> we use (idx + k)  % 4 for detecting cyclic cases
21            """
22            if c == 'L':
23                idx = (idx + 3) % 4
24            elif c == 'R':
25                idx = (idx + 1) % 4
26            elif c == 'G':
27                x = x + dirs[idx][0]
28                y = y + dirs[idx][1]
29        return (x == 0 and y ==0) or idx !=0

2-10) Maximum Length of Subarray With Positive Product

1# LC 1567 Maximum Length of Subarray With Positive Product
2
3# V0
4class Solution:
5    def getMaxLen(self, nums):
6        first_neg, zero = None, -1
7        mx = neg = 0
8        for i,v in enumerate(nums):
9            if v == 0:
10                first_neg, zero, neg = None, i, 0
11                continue
12            if v < 0:
13                neg += 1
14                if first_neg == None:
15                    first_neg = i
16            j = zero if not neg % 2 else first_neg if first_neg != None else 10**9
17            mx = max(mx, i-j)
18        return mx
19
20# V0'
21# IDEA : 2 POINTERS
22class Solution:
23    def getMaxLen(self, nums):
24        res = 0
25        k = -1 # most recent 0
26        j = -1 # first negative after most recent 0
27        cnt = 0 # count of negatives after most recent 0
28        for i, n in enumerate(nums):
29            if n == 0:
30                k = i
31                j = i
32                cnt = 0
33            elif n < 0:
34                cnt += 1
35                if cnt % 2 == 0:
36                    res = max(res, i - k)
37                else:
38                    if cnt == 1:
39                        j = i
40                    else:
41                        res = max(res, i - j)        
42            else:
43                if cnt % 2 == 0:
44                    res = max(res, i - k)
45                else:
46                    res = max(res, i - j)
47        return res

2-11) Corporate Flight Bookings

1# LC 1109. Corporate Flight Bookings
2# V1
3# IDEA : ARRAY + prefix sum
4# https://leetcode.com/problems/corporate-flight-bookings/discuss/328856/JavaC%2B%2BPython-Sweep-Line
5# IDEA :
6# Set the change of seats for each day.
7# If booking = [i, j, k],
8# it needs k more seat on ith day,
9# and we don't need these seats on j+1th day.
10# We accumulate these changes then we have the result that we want.
11# Complexity
12# Time O(booking + N) for one pass on bookings
13# Space O(N) for the result
14class Solution:
15    def corpFlightBookings(self, bookings, n):
16        res = [0] * (n + 1)
17        for i, j, k in bookings:
18            res[i - 1] += k
19            res[j] -= k
20        for i in range(1, n):
21            res[i] += res[i - 1]
22        return res[:-1]
23
24# V1''
25# IDEA : ARRAY
26# https://leetcode.com/problems/corporate-flight-bookings/discuss/328893/Short-python-solution
27# IDEA : Simply use two arrays to keep track of how many bookings are added for every flight.
28class Solution:
29    def corpFlightBookings(self, bookings: List[List[int]], n: int) -> List[int]:        
30        opens = [0]*n
31        closes = [0]*n
32        
33        for e in bookings:
34            opens[e[0]-1] += e[2]
35            closes[e[1]-1] += e[2]
36            
37        ret, tmp = [0]*n, 0
38        for i in range(n):
39            tmp += opens[i]
40            ret[i] = tmp
41            tmp -= closes[i]
42            
43        return ret

2-12) First Missing Positive

1# LC 41. First Missing Positive
2# V1'
3# IDEA :  Index as a hash key.
4# https://leetcode.com/problems/first-missing-positive/solution/
5# /doc/pic/first-missing-positive.png
6class Solution:
7    def firstMissingPositive(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
8        n = len(nums)
9
10        # Base case.
11        if 1 not in nums:
12            return 1
13
14        # Replace negative numbers, zeros,
15        # and numbers larger than n by 1s.
16        # After this convertion nums will contain
17        # only positive numbers.
18        for i in range(n):
19            if nums[i] <= 0 or nums[i] > n:
20                nums[i] = 1
21
22        # Use index as a hash key and number sign as a presence detector.
23        # For example, if nums[1] is negative that means that number `1`
24        # is present in the array.
25        # If nums[2] is positive - number 2 is missing.
26        for i in range(n):
27            a = abs(nums[i])
28            # If you meet number a in the array - change the sign of a-th element.
29            # Be careful with duplicates : do it only once.
30            if a == n:
31                nums[0] = - abs(nums[0])
32            else:
33                nums[a] = - abs(nums[a])
34
35        # Now the index of the first positive number
36        # is equal to first missing positive.
37        for i in range(1, n):
38            if nums[i] > 0:
39                return i
40
41        if nums[0] > 0:
42            return n
43
44        return n + 1

1// java
2// LC 41. First Missing Positive
3// V0
4// IDEA: CYCLIC SORT
5/**
6 * Cyclic Sort Pattern:
7 * Place each positive number x at its "correct" index (x - 1)
8 *
9 * Key idea:
10 * - For a valid positive integer x (1 <= x <= n), it should be at index x-1
11 * - Example: number 3 should be at nums[2], number 1 should be at nums[0]
12 *
13 * Algorithm:
14 * 1. For each position i, keep swapping nums[i] to its correct position
15 *    until nums[i] is already at the right place or out of range
16 * 2. After sorting, scan for the first index i where nums[i] != i + 1
17 * 3. That index + 1 is the first missing positive
18 *
19 * Example: nums = [3, 4, -1, 1]
20 * Step 1: Place 3 at index 2 → [-1, 4, 3, 1]
21 * Step 2: Place 4 at index 3 → [-1, 1, 3, 4]
22 * Step 3: Place 1 at index 0 → [1, -1, 3, 4]
23 * Step 4: Scan → nums[1] = -1 ≠ 2, return 2
24 *
25 * Time: O(N) - each element is swapped at most once
26 * Space: O(1) - in-place sorting
27 */
28public int firstMissingPositive(int[] nums) {
29    int n = nums.length;
30
31    // 1. "Cyclic Sort": Place each number x at index x - 1
32    // Example: nums[i] = 3 should be at nums[2]
33    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
34        while (nums[i] > 0 && nums[i] <= n && nums[nums[i] - 1] != nums[i]) {
35            // Swap nums[i] with the element at its target index
36            int temp = nums[nums[i] - 1];
37            nums[nums[i] - 1] = nums[i];
38            nums[i] = temp;
39        }
40    }
41
42    // 2. Scan for the first index where the number is wrong
43    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
44        if (nums[i] != i + 1) {
45            return i + 1; // Found the missing positive!
46        }
47    }
48
49    // 3. If all numbers 1 to n are present, the answer is n + 1
50    return n + 1;
51}

2-13) Increasing Triplet Subsequence

1# LC 334 Increasing Triplet Subsequence
2# V0
3# IDEA : MAINTAIN var first, second
4#        AND GO THROUGH nums to check if there exists x (on the right hand side of a, b )
5#        such that x > second > first
6class Solution(object):
7    def increasingTriplet(self, nums):
8        """
9        NOTE !!! we init first, second as POSITIVE float('inf')
10        """
11        first = float('inf')
12        second = float('inf')
13        # loop with normal ordering
14        for num in nums:
15            if num <= first:     # min num
16                first = num
17            elif num <= second:  # 2nd min num
18                second = num
19            else:                # 3rd min num
20                return True      
21        return False

2-14) First Missing Positive

1# LC 41. First Missing Positive
2# V0
3# IDEA : for loop + while loop + problem understanding
4class Solution:
5    def firstMissingPositive(self, nums):
6        for i, n in enumerate(nums):
7            if n < 0:
8                continue
9            else:
10                while n <= len(nums) and n > 0:
11                    tmp = nums[n-1]
12                    nums[n-1] = float('inf')
13                    n = tmp
14        for i in range(len(nums)):
15            if nums[i] != float('inf'):
16                return i+1
17            
18        return len(nums)+1

2-15) Rotate Array

1# LC 189. Rotate Array
2# V0
3# IDEA : pop + insert
4class Solution(object):
5    def rotate(self, nums, k):
6        _len = len(nums)
7        k = k % _len
8        while k > 0:
9            tmp = nums.pop(-1)
10            nums.insert(0, tmp)
11            k -= 1
12
13# V0'
14# IDEA : SLICE (in place)
15class Solution(object):
16    def rotate(self, nums, k):
17        # edge case
18        if k == 0 or not nums or len(nums) == 1:
19            return nums
20        ### NOTE this
21        k = k % len(nums)
22        if k == 0:
23            return nums
24        """
25        NOTE this !!!!
26        """
27        nums[:k], nums[k:] = nums[-k:], nums[:-k]
28        return nums

1// java
2// LC 189. Rotate Array
3// V0
4// IDEA: REVERSE ARRAY (3-reverse trick)
5/**
6 * The 3-reverse trick:
7 * 1. Reverse the entire array
8 * 2. Reverse the first k elements
9 * 3. Reverse the remaining elements
10 *
11 * Example: nums = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7], k = 3
12 * Step 1: Reverse entire array → [7,6,5,4,3,2,1]
13 * Step 2: Reverse first k=3 elements → [5,6,7,4,3,2,1]
14 * Step 3: Reverse remaining elements → [5,6,7,1,2,3,4]
15 *
16 * Time: O(N) - each element is reversed twice
17 * Space: O(1) - in-place rotation
18 */
19public void rotate(int[] nums, int k) {
20    if (nums == null || nums.length <= 1)
21        return;
22
23    int n = nums.length;
24    // Step 1: Handle cases where k > n
25    k = k % n;
26    if (k == 0)
27        return;
28
29    // Step 2: Apply the 3-reverse trick
30    // 1. Reverse the whole array
31    reverse(nums, 0, n - 1);
32    // 2. Reverse the first k elements (0 to k-1)
33    reverse(nums, 0, k - 1);
34    // 3. Reverse the rest (k to n-1)
35    reverse(nums, k, n - 1);
36}
37
38private void reverse(int[] nums, int start, int end) {
39    while (start < end) {
40        int temp = nums[start];
41        nums[start] = nums[end];
42        nums[end] = temp;
43        start++;
44        end--;
45    }
46}

2-16) Flatten 2D Vector

1# LC 251. Flatten 2D Vector
2# V0
3# IDEA : ARRAY OP
4class Vector2D:
5
6    def __init__(self, v):
7        # We need to iterate over the 2D vector, getting all the integers
8        # out of it and putting them into the nums list.
9        self.nums = []
10        for inner_list in v:
11            for num in inner_list:
12                self.nums.append(num)
13        # We'll keep position 1 behind the next number to return.
14        self.position = -1
15
16    def next(self):
17        # Move up to the current element and return it.
18        self.position += 1
19        return self.nums[self.position]
20
21    def hasNext(self):
22        # If the next position is a valid index of nums, return True.
23        return self.position + 1 < len(self.nums)

2-17) Maximize Distance to Closest Person

1// java
2// LC 849. Maximize Distance to Closest Person
3
4// V0-1
5// IDEA (fixed by gpt)
6/**
7*  IDEA :
8*
9*  Explanation of the Code:
10*    1.  Initial Setup:
11*        •   lastOccupied keeps track of the index of the last seat occupied by a person.
12*        •   maxDistance is initialized to 0 to store the maximum distance found.
13*
14*    2.  Iterate Through the Array:
15*        •   When a seat is occupied (seats[i] == 1):
16*        •   If it’s the first occupied seat, calculate the distance from the start of the array to this seat (i).
17*        •   Otherwise, calculate the middle distance between the current and the last occupied seat using (i - lastOccupied) / 2.
18*
19*    3.  Check the Last Segment:
20*        •   If the last seat is empty, calculate the distance from the last occupied seat to the end of the array (seats.length - 1 - lastOccupied).
21*
22*    4.  Return the Maximum Distance:
23*        •   The value of maxDistance at the end of the loop is the answer.
24*
25*
26* Example :
27*  input : seats = [1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 1]
28*
29*   Execution Steps:
30*    1.  First occupied seat at index 0 → Distance to start = 0.
31*    2.  Second occupied seat at index 4 → Middle distance = (4 - 0) / 2 = 2.
32*    3.  Third occupied seat at index 6 → Middle distance = (6 - 4) / 2 = 1.
33*    4.  No empty seats after the last occupied seat.
34*    5.  maxDistance = 2.
35*
36*  output:  2
37*
38*/
39/**
40*  Cases
41*
42*  Case 1)  0001  ( all "0" till meat first "1")
43*  Case 2)  1001001 (all "0" are enclosed by "1")
44*  Case 3)  1001000 (there are "0" that NOT enclosed by "1" on the right hand side)
45*
46*/
47public int maxDistToClosest_0_1(int[] seats) {
48    int maxDistance = 0;
49    int lastOccupied = -1;
50
51    // Traverse the array to calculate maximum distances
52    for (int i = 0; i < seats.length; i++) {
53        /** NOTE !!! handle the seat val == 1 cases */
54        if (seats[i] == 1) {
55            if (lastOccupied == -1) {
56                // Handle the case where the `first` occupied seat is found
57                /**
58                 * NOTE !!!
59                 *
60                 *  for handling below case:
61                 *
62                 *    e.g. :  0001
63                 *
64                 *  (so, elements are all "0" till first visit "1")
65                 *  in this case, we still can get put a person to seat, and get distance
66                 *
67                 */
68                maxDistance = i; // Distance from the start to the first occupied seat
69            } else {
70                // Calculate the distance to the closest person for the middle segment
71                /** NOTE !!! need to divided by 2, since the person need to seat at `middle` seat */
72                maxDistance = Math.max(maxDistance, (i - lastOccupied) / 2);
73            }
74            lastOccupied = i;
75        }
76    }
77
78    // Handle the case where the last segment is empty
79   /**
80    *  NOTE !!!
81    *
82    *   the condition is actually quite straightforward,
83    *   just need to check if the last element in array is "0"
84    *   if is "0", means the array is NOT enclosed by "1"
85    *   then we need to handle such case
86    *   (example as below)
87    *
88    *   e.g.  100010000
89    *
90    */
91    if (seats[seats.length - 1] == 0) {
92        maxDistance = Math.max(maxDistance, seats.length - 1 - lastOccupied);
93    }
94
95    return maxDistance;
96}
← Back to Cheat Sheets Edit on GitHub →
← Advanced String AlgorithmsArray Overlap Explaination →

CS_basics - Computer Science Fundamentals & Interview Preparation

GitHub • Documentation • Report Issues
